channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Women Judges in Tanzania Strengthen Efforts to Protect GBV Survivors
item.description: In Dodoma, women judges across Tanzania have once again affirmed their strong commitment to fight gender-based violence (GBV) and protect victims more effectively. The reaffirmation came during the 20...

item.title: AI Tools Challenge Traditional Indian IT Outsourcing Model
item.description: On 4th February, the $300 billion Indian IT sector experienced a notable shake-up as the stock index for IT firms fell almost 6% after Anthropic launched its Claude Cowork agentic plugin. This AI plug...

item.title: Kunle Afolayan Flags Diesel Costs Affecting KAP Film Village Operations
item.description: Nollywood filmmaker Kunle Afolayan don express serious wahala over the high cost of diesel wey dey affect him business operations. According to him, him KAP Film Village and Resort dey spend around ₦1...

item.title: Oburu Clarifies UDA-ODM Collaboration Predates March 7 Ruto-Raila Agreement
item.description: ODM leader Oburu Oginga has clarified in a joint parliamentary session that the broad-based political framework between UDA and ODM started long before the March 7, 2025, 10-point agenda deal between ...

item.title: The Importance of Independent Journalism in the Digital Age
item.description: In today’s media landscape, where everyone with a smartphone can broadcast their own story, the value of journalism is often underestimated. Platforms promising ‘democratised’ media made it seem like ...

item.title: UAE ensures six-month supply of essential goods amid regional conflict
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has declared that it maintains a six-month strategic reserve of key essential goods in response to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. The Ministry of Economy and Tourism has i...

item.title: Lululemon fined $703,000 for sending marketing emails without unsubscribe option
item.description: Athleisure brand Lululemon has been fined $703,000 by the Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) after sending over 370,000 emails that breached spam regulations. These emails, which inc...

item.title: Seventh fraternity member surrenders in connection with maritime student’s hazing death
item.description: Another member of the Tau Gamma Phi fraternity, Dar Sabilona, 26, surrendered to the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group-National Capital Region (CIDG-NCR) at Camp Crame in Quezon City on Monda...

item.title: Shadow Fleet Maintains Oil Transit Amid Strait of Hormuz Conflict
item.description: Since the outbreak of the conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran on February 28, 2026, the Strait of Hormuz has seen a dramatic collapse in oil tanker traffic, dropping by over 90%. Ir...

item.title: Food Inflation in Canada: Drivers, Risks, and Outlook for 2026
item.description: Canada currently has the highest food inflation among G7 countries, with rates reaching 7.3 per cent and remaining elevated since mid-2025. A report from Desjardins highlights that grocery prices are ...

item.title: Tanzanian businessman Mo Dewji maintains title as Africa’s youngest billionaire with $2.1 billion net worth
item.description: Mohammed Dewji, famously known as Mo Dewji, has continued to hold his position as Africa’s youngest billionaire, according to the latest Forbes Magazine report on Africa’s richest individuals. With an...

item.title: Tanzania to present efforts on promoting justice and equality for women at UN session
item.description: Dr Dorothy Gwajima, the Minister of Community Development, Gender, Women, and Special Groups, go represent Tanzania for the United Nations 70th session wey dey focus on the Status of Women worldwide. ...

item.title: Six Members of a Bungoma Family Die in Webuye Road Accident
item.description: On Monday night, a tragic road accident in Webuye Sub-County left 15 people dead, including six members of a single family from Mihuu Ward. The incident began when two boda boda operators collided, re...

item.title: KRA Introduces Body Cameras for Customs Officers at JKIA to Improve Transparency and Accountability
item.description: Nairobi — Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) has officially introduced body-worn cameras for Customs and Border Control officers at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA). This initiative is part of KR...

item.title: Elon Musk reaches $839 billion net worth, on track to become first trillionaire
item.description: Elon Musk has reached an unprecedented net worth of $839 billion in March 2026, making him the wealthiest individual ever recorded, according to Forbes. His fortune has surged by roughly $500 billion ...

item.title: Sundowns Claim Top Spot After 2-0 Win Over Orbit College
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns secured a solid 2-0 win against Orbit College FC at Olympia Park Stadium on Tuesday night, propelling them to the summit of the Betway Premiership standings with 47 points, three ahe...

item.title: Southern Quebec Schools Close Early Due to Forecasted Freezing Rain
item.description: School boards across southern Quebec have announced pre-emptive closures on Wednesday due to a major freezing rain storm forecasted by Environment Canada. The Centre de services scolaire de Montréal (...

item.title: Ombudsman moves to freeze properties of Revilla and Discaya amid graft cases
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman announced it will request writs of preliminary attachment to place the properties of former Senator Ramon “Bong” Revilla Jr. and contractor Sarah Discaya under the custody ...

item.title: Canadian First World War soldiers’ remains returned by Philadelphia museum for proper burial
item.description: A Philadelphia medical history museum has returned partial remains of Canadian soldiers from the First World War, ensuring they are properly interred in the graves of the men from whom they were taken...

item.title: Public Opinion Opposes Trump’s Military Action in Iran Amid Weak National Narrative
item.description: President Donald Trump's war against Iran stands out historically for its lack of public support. Unlike previous U.S.-led regime change conflicts, which often gained broad backing through compelling ...
